div.p: Zero-copy slot claiming with sequence number coordination
Load current producer position. Relaxed ordering - no synchronization needed yet.
Variables: cpp uint64_t seq = sequence[pos & mask].load(acquire)
Acquire ordering ensures we see all prior writes. Slot 5 has sequence 5 → slot is empty, ready for producer
Variables: cpp int64_t diff = seq - pos // 5 - 5 = 0
diff == 0: Slot expects this position → READY diff < 0: Slot is behind → QUEUE FULL diff > 0: Another producer claimed → RETRY
Variables: cpp bool success = tail.compare_exchange_weak( pos, // expected: 5 pos + 1, // desired: 6 relaxed, relaxed)
CAS succeeds → We own slot at position 5 CAS fails → Another producer beat us, retry from Step 1
Variables: cpp size_t slot_idx = pos & mask; // 5 & 7 = 5 memcpy(slots[slot_idx], data, size);
Copy message to claimed slot. Data not yet visible to consumers.

ol.li: Claimed slot via atomic CAS on tail
Published via sequence update (pos + 1) Consumer sees sequence[slot] == pos + 1 → data is ready

div.p: Slot sequence is behind position. Example: pos=5, seq=3 Consumer hasn't caught up. return false (caller applies backpressure)
Another producer claimed but hasn't written. Example: pos=5, seq=7 Reload tail and retry
Another producer's CAS succeeded first. pos updated by CAS, retry loop

g.text: ref_count | msg_size | timestamp | topic_hash | seq | depth | flags
